papers.title: ClinicBot: A Guideline-Grounded Clinical Chatbot with Prioritized Evidence RAG and Verifiable Citations
papers.innovation: A novel chatbot architecture for high-stakes medical contexts, employing Prioritized Evidence RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and verifiable citations to explicitly ground answers in official guidelines, effectively mitigating LLM hallucination.
papers.benchmarks: Improved accuracy and verifiability compared to standard LLMs by grounding responses in clinical guidelines and providing explicit citations, essential for high-stakes medical applications.
papers.use_case: Clinical decision support, medical information retrieval, building trustworthy and verifiable LLM applications in sensitive domains.

papers.title: Towards Multi-Agent Autonomous Reasoning in Hydrodynamics
papers.innovation: Proposes a multi-agent system paradigm to overcome the inherent limitations of single-agent LLM-driven scientific workflows, specifically addressing context window accumulation, complex routing, and tool use for enhanced autonomous reasoning.
papers.benchmarks: Aims to significantly improve scalability, complexity handling, and overall reasoning capabilities compared to single-agent LLM systems by distributing tasks and managing context more effectively.
papers.use_case: Scientific discovery and simulation (e.g., hydrodynamics), complex problem-solving with LLMs, designing scalable and robust AI systems, mitigating LLM context window constraints in advanced applications.

papers.title: Effect-Transparent Governance for AI Workflow Architectures: Semantic Preservation, Expressive Minimality, and Decidability Boundaries
papers.innovation: Presents a machine-checked formalization (using Rocq 8.19) of governed AI workflow architectures, proving that effect-level governance can be imposed without sacrificing computational expressivity. This establishes foundational principles for verifiable AI system design.
papers.benchmarks: Formal proofs demonstrating semantic preservation, expressive minimality, and decidability boundaries for governed AI workflows, providing strong guarantees about system behavior and safety properties.
papers.use_case: Designing secure, auditable, and reliable AI workflow architectures, implementing AI governance in safety-critical systems, formal verification of AI system properties, ensuring compliance and transparency in AI deployment.
